title: nautilocalyx pictus
intro: Nautilocalyx pictus is a species of plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage family. It is a shrubby perennial plant native to tropical Africa, and its name refers to the Nautilus shell-like shape of its calyx. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Nautilocalyx pictus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function.
intro_study: According to Phytotherapy Research: PTR, nautilocalyx pictus exhibits immunostimulatory activity, particularly its insoluble fraction, which can potentially be used as an immunostimulatory agent in humans.
